primary_diagnosis.name: Anxiety-driven (psychogenic) polydipsia with mild hyperglycaemia, on a background of latent autoimmune diabetes in adults (LADA); no ketoacidosis

pathophysiology_summary: LADA is a slowly progressive autoimmune (GAD-antibody-positive) diabetes that retains residual beta-cell function for months to years and is often initially managed with diet and oral agents. Marked hyperglycaemia with ketosis (DKA) can eventually occur, but requires substantial insulin deficiency. Here, a minor dietary slip caused mild hyperglycaemia; fear then drove the patient to drink ~3 L of water, producing secondary polyuria that reinforced the panic. The home glucose (~12 mmol/L) is moderate and the blood ketones are negative, and there are no DKA features (vomiting, abdominal pain, Kussmaul breathing, drowsiness, fruity breath). Telehealth CAN meaningfully risk-stratify here using the patient's own glucometer and ketone strips. The task is to exclude DKA/HHS with these tools and the symptom screen, then de-escalate the fear-driven overdrinking with a robust DKA safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Mild hyperglycaemia after a dietary slip with fear-driven overdrinking and secondary polyuria.
Autoimmune diabetes can develop DKA; screen ketones and DKA symptoms — negative here.
Excluded by only moderate glucose, alert mentation and good hydration.
Home glucose ~12 mmol/L with negative ketones (DI-001), no DKA symptoms (DI-002), polyuria explained by ~3 L fear-driven water intake (DI-003) — not a hyperglycaemic emergency.
Negative blood ketones and no DKA symptoms — DKA excluded now, but ketones must be rechecked if symptoms change.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: reassure using the home glucose/ketone data and symptom screen, reduce fear-driven overdrinking, routine GP review of glycaemic management, and a clear DKA safety-net (recheck ketones; ED if ketones rise, vomiting, drowsiness, or glucose very high). ED dispatch for a moderate glucose with negative ketones is over-triage.

red_flags.description: Significant hyperglycaemia with ketosis (rising blood/urine ketones, glucose reading HIGH/>25)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Signals evolving DKA in autoimmune diabetes.

red_flags.description: DKA symptoms — vomiting, abdominal pain, Kussmaul breathing, drowsiness/confusion, fruity breath

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Being pulled into the 'diabetic coma' fear and dispatching to ED for a moderate glucose with negative ketones.
Failing to use the patient's own glucometer/ketone strips, which allow genuine risk-stratification of DKA remotely.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Laboratory venous glucose, blood gas/pH and formal ketone measurement
Assessment of true hydration/observations beyond report
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure using home glucose/ketones + DKA symptom screen; reduce overdrinking; routine GP review; DKA safety-net (recheck ketones, ED if ketosis/vomiting/drowsiness/very high glucose).
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Dismissing without checking ketones/symptoms could miss evolving DKA; over-escalating a moderate glucose reinforces health anxiety.
